frontmatter.title: You are a structured-output adapter for Cognee.
frontmatter.summary: You are a structured-output adapter for Cognee. Return ONLY one valid JSON object. No markdown fences, no commentary. The JSON MUST validate against this Pydantic JSON Schema for KnowledgeGraph: { "$defs": { "Edge": { "description": "Edge in a knowledge graph.", "properties": { "source_node_id": { "title": "Source Node Id", "type": "string" }, "target_node_id": { "title": "Target Node Id", "type": "string" }, "relationship_name": { "title": "Relationship Name", "type": "string" }, "properties": { "anyOf": [ { "type": "object" }, { "type": "null" } ], "default": null, "description": "A dictionary of properties associated with the edge.", "title": "Properties" } }, "required": [ "source_node_id", "target_node_id", "relationship_name" ], "title": "Edge", "type": "object" }, "Node": { "description": "Node in a knowledge graph.", "properties": { "id": { "title": "Id", "type": "string" }, "name": { "title": "Name", "type": "string" }, "type": { "title": "Type", "type": "string" }, "description": { "title": "Description", "type": "string" }, "properties": { "anyOf": [ { "type": "object" }, { "type": "null" } ], "default": null, "description": "A dictionary of properties associated with th
commands_run:
python - <<'PY'
import json
from collections import Counter
nodes={}
with open('output/cognee_codex_full_bundle/nodes.jsonl') as f:
    for line in f:
        obj=json.loads(line)
        nodes[obj['cognee_id']]=obj
used=set()
rels=Counter()
missing=set()
count=0
with open('output/cognee_codex_full_bundle/edges.jsonl') as f:
    for line in f:
        e=json.loads(line)
        count+=1
        used.add(e['source_id']); used.add(e['target_id']); rels[e['relationship']]+=1
for uid in used:
    if uid not in nodes:
        missing.add(uid)
print('edge_count',count)
print('used_nodes',len(used))
print('missing_nodes',len(missing))
print('top_relationships', rels.most_common(20))
if missing:
    print('sample_missing', list(sorted(missing))[:50])
PY

python -c $'import json
from collections import Counter
nodes={}
for line in open("output/cognee_codex_full_bundle/nodes.jsonl"):
    obj=json.loads(line); nodes[obj["cognee_id"]]=obj
used=set(); rels=Counter(); count=0
for line in open("output/cognee_codex_full_bundle/edges.jsonl"):
    e=json.loads(line); count+=1; used.add(e["source_id"]); used.add(e["target_id"]); rels[e["relationship"]]+=1
missing=sorted(uid for uid in used if uid not in nodes)
print("edge_count", count)
print("used_nodes", len(used))
print("missing_nodes", len(missing))
print("top_relationships", rels.most_common(20))
print("sample_missing", missing[:20])'

turns.text: I’m reading `output/cognee_codex_full_bundle/edges.jsonl` and will convert its contents into a schema-valid knowledge graph JSON object.

I’ve confirmed the input is an edge list with opaque typed IDs. I’m checking whether the bundle also includes node records so the returned graph can have meaningful node names and descriptions instead of only raw edge endpoints.

I have enough context to synthesize the output. I’m now normalizing the Cognee bundle into the required `nodes` and `edges` shape, with simple node types and descriptions pulled from the node records where available.

turns.text: {"cmd":"python -c $'import json, collections\\nnodes={}\\nfor line in open(\"output/cognee_codex_full_bundle/nodes.jsonl\"):\\n    o=json.loads(line); nodes[o[\"cognee_id\"]]=o\\nctype=collections.Counter(); cname=collections.Counter(); pairs=collections.Counter()\\nfor line in open(\"output/cognee_codex_full_bundle/edges.jsonl\"):\\n    e=json.loads(line)\\n    s=nodes[e[\"source_id\"]]; t=nodes[e[\"target_id\"]]\\n    st=s[\"metadata\"].get(\"research_node_type\",\"Unknown\")\\n    tt=t[\"metadata\"].get(\"research_node_type\",\"Unknown\")\\n    ctype[(st,e[\"relationship\"],tt)] += 1\\n    if st in {\"ResearchField\",\"ResearchTopic\",\"MethodologicalConcept\",\"ApproachFamily\",\"TechnicalTerm\"} and tt in {\"ResearchField\",\"ResearchTopic\",\"MethodologicalConcept\",\"ApproachFamily\",\"TechnicalTerm\"}:\\n        pairs[(s[\"name\"],e[\"relationship\"],t[\"name\"])] += 1\\nprint(\"top type triples:\")\\nfor k,v in ctype.most_common(20): print(v,k)\\nprint(\"top concept pairs:\")\\nfor k,v in pairs.most_common(20): print(v,k)\\n'","workdir":"/Users/neo/Developer/Projects/LLM-Wiki","yield_time_ms":1000,"max_output_tokens":4000}
